name: Phil Wilson
abstract: Phillips Elder Wilson, Jr. (born January 19, 1937) is a jazz trombonist, arranger, and teacher.

interviews.title: Phil Wilson interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, September 27, 2023

concerts.label: Woody Herman and His Thundering Herd: 40th Anniversary Concert
Newport Jazz Festival: Mel Torme with Herb Pomeroy Big Band and Gerry Mulligan/ Gerry Mulligan Sextet

quotes.text: I loved Bill Harris and I was hugely influenced by him. When I was in high school I had a band at Phillips Exeter, believe it or not, playing all the charts I wanted to play. And I wrote out [scats] “Everywhere,” which was a big feature for Bill Harris, 1946 Carnegie Hall concert [scats] and the beautiful ending and all this sort of thing. He had a sound that was as luscious, Bill Harris — Bill Harris — Bill Watrous years later but he wasn’t at obviously the same level playing the horn. He was more by the seat of his pants, so was Bill actually but he had had a little more experience.
quotes.caption: Recalling Bill Harris's 1946 Carnegie Hall performance
quotes.doc_title: Phil Wilson interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, September 27, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: I was doing a lot of those things, like stage-band clinics with a really nice group. Sometimes Michael Moore would be the bass player. Phil Wilson from Berklee [College of Music]. John LaPorta. ... That would be the faculty.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Woody Herman
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Was Phil Wilson there, at the time? [INTERVIEWEE] On this 1963 album? ... Well, I think the ones I mentioned were the strongest ones... But there were people that came in and out of the band.

about_quotes.speaker: Ernie Watts
about_quotes.evidence: The manager of the band... called Phil Wilson, who was a teacher, a trombone teacher at Berklee, and asked who he would recommend to come out with Buddy’s band... So I was the kid that got recommended
about_quotes.doc_title: Ernie Watts interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 11, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Durham
about_quotes.evidence: INTERVIEWER mentions Phil Wilson; INTERVIEWEE responds generally about 'those guys' and 'whoever comes along now' without indicating personal interaction or specific knowledge of Phil Wilson.

about_quotes.speaker: Ed Soph
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] All right. I’m going to be careful how I look at my drummer tonight. You know I was talking to Phil Wilson about you. [INTERVIEWEE] Oh wow.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ed Soph interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 9, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Hal Espinosa
about_quotes.evidence: Jimmy Campbell was the drummer the whole time I was on Woody’s band... and then Phil Wilson came in too I think.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hal Espinosa interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, March 20, 2024
